vllm.title: [Setup] Enable `TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST` for selecting target GPUs
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fixes #1070
The TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST env variable is a standard place to specify the target GPUs one wants to build a PyTorch project for. This PR enables using the variable in our setup.py. This will be especially useful for those who build vLLM images for specific GPUs.

vllm.title: Add comments on RoPE initialization
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Added more comments that can be useful for understanding the differences from HF.

vllm.title: Allocate more shared memory to attention kernel
vllm.author: Yard1
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Makes use of additional shared memory present on compute capability >=7.0 cards to support longer context length in the attention kernel.
See https://stackoverflow.com/questions/63757245/using-maximum-shared-memory-in-cuda for details.
